marketState: neutral-to-bearish - fair-valued front month, weak seasonal/storage backdrop
summary: NG is trading at $2.874, essentially fair value (spread -$0.043 vs the $2.917 model fair) but well below its seasonal median for this time of year (-12.2pp; YTD -15.6% vs -3.4% median), and 16-year seasonality shows sub-50% odds of being higher in 1/2/4 weeks with a typical -3.1% 4-week drift, though reliability is flagged Low this week (50pp spread). Storage printed a bearish surprise (+72 Bcf vs forecast, +184 Bcf above the 5-year average), reinforcing an oversupplied backdrop, while managed money remains net short (-60,295 contracts, 35th percentile) without being at an extreme crowded level. Near-term weather demand is running hot (+21.4% vs 5y, rangePos 0.92, near the top of its range) which is short-term supportive, but the 8-16 day outlook has already cooled well below normal (-9.4%, rangePos 0.31) and the 24h demand reading is falling fast (-9.0). Technicals are uniformly bearish (bearish trend plus a 50<200 death cross) across every timeframe from 5-minute to weekly, though the daily is already stretched below its lower Bollinger band, arguing against chasing either direction right now.

longTerm.entry: $2.95 - $3.05 (sell into a rally toward the fair-value/resistance zone)
longTerm.rationale: Seasonality shows below-median pricing with a historically negative 4-week drift (-3.1%) and sub-50% odds of higher prices in 1/2/4-week windows; storage is running a bearish surprise (+72 Bcf, +184 Bcf above the 5y average), an oversupplied backdrop; COT shows managed money net short (35th percentile) without extreme crowding, leaving room to add to shorts.
longTerm.invalidation: A sustained close back above the plus21D fair-value midpoint (~$3.04) or an 8-16D weather demand swing back above the 5-year normal would undercut the bearish seasonal/storage thesis.

shortTerm.rationale: Near-term (1-7D) weather demand is hot (+21.4% vs 5y) which is supportive, but this is offset by a fading 8-16D outlook, a uniformly bearish technical trend with a death cross on every timeframe from 5m to 1w, and a front price that is already essentially fair value (spread only -$0.043) - no clean directional edge either way.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for either (a) daily RSI reclaiming above ~45 with MACD histogram turning positive to confirm a bounce off the lower Bollinger band for a tactical long back toward fair value ($2.92-2.95), or (b) a confirmed close below the recent 1d low with the 8-16D weather demand outlook staying sub-normal, to confirm a continuation short.

keyDrivers: Seasonality below-median with negative 4-week drift (-3.1%) but flagged Low reliability
Bearish storage surprise: +72 Bcf vs forecast, +184 Bcf above 5y average
Near-term weather hot (+21.4% vs 5y) but 8-16D outlook already fading (-9.4%)
Price essentially FAIR vs models across all horizons (spread within a few cents)
Uniform bearish technical trend and death cross (50<200 EMA) on every timeframe 5m-1w
risks: Weather forecasts can flip quickly, especially cooling-season demand swings
Low seasonality reliability this week (50pp spread) means the median-move signal could fail
Futures curve prices a large winter risk premium (Dec +$0.90, Jan +$1.35 vs front) - an early cold snap or storage tightening could spark a sharp squeeze higher
